TextENGNew: "Umm, excuse me..."
A girl's voice reached her from the waiting room on the other side of the reception window.
On-duty nurse Hiranuma stopped writing her report for the fire department, blinked her eyes — strained from concentration — and looked up.
"Ah... Ozaki-san."
"...Good evening."
It was a girl named Nagisa Ozaki, whose face she had grown familiar with over the past several months.
The girl gave a small bow, hiding her mouth behind the bouquet she was holding in both hands.
Hiranuma smiled warmly and returned a friendly "Good evening."
"Visiting again today?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2004. ...It must be hard coming all this way every week from so far off."
"Oh — not at all.@k@vS11/35/420500004. ...I'm doing it because I want to."
Casting a shy upward glance at Hiranuma's face, Nagisa added the words with the tension just slightly released.

The ribbons tied on either side suited her well, and her slightly upturned eyes gave her a spirited impression. There was a refined quality to her appearance, and her pretty blouse carried a modest air of elegance.
Her expression had a faint sullenness, a slight furrowing of the brow. But Hiranuma had recently come to understand that this was simply an involuntary reaction stemming from her shy nature.
"...Is Kouhei-kun available to talk right now?"
"Yes, I think so.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2006. He'd just woken up from a nap when we took his temperature a little while ago."
"Is that right?@k@vS11/35/420500007. ...Good."
Nagisa's expression softened with relief, and her eyes fell to the colorful bouquet in her arms.
Cosmos flowers — their vivid pink and green bright as freshly cut blooms.
...A sweet scent seemed to drift even through the reception window, and Hiranuma leaned in to look closer, her face breaking into an involuntary smile.
"That's quite a lot of flowers.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2008. Were they expensive?"

"Oh — no.@k@vS11/35/420500009. ...These were growing in the gardening club's flower bed at school. They finally bloomed, and I wanted to show them to Kouhei-kun directly.@k@vS11/35/420500010. ...Is that all right?"
"Of course it is."
"Thank you very much."
With those words, Nagisa bowed politely.
She was still a little stiff in how she interacted, but when she had first started visiting, she had barely said anything beyond "yes" and "no." By that measure, the progress was remarkable.
"Oh, but you'll need a vase.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2011. Just a moment.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2012. I'll go get one and we can head up together."
"...Ah, sorry to trouble you.@k@vS11/35/420500013. Please."
Nagisa nodded with a gentle smile.
That she would rely on someone so naturally and easily was another recent change. Thinking this, Hiranuma gave her a reassuring look, rose from her seat, and headed toward the back.
The hallway felt somewhat dim, perhaps because of the late afternoon hour.
Dinner time was also approaching, so foot traffic coming and going was sparse.
"Umm... a lady patient on the first floor told me an alarm went off earlier.@k@vS11/35/420500015. ...Did something happen?"
"Ah, it looks like the fire alarm had a small malfunction.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2014. I checked straight away, and there was no abnormality, so there's nothing to worry about."
"Oh, I see.@k@vS11/35/420500017. I was a little startled when I heard."
"So was I. Sorry for the worry."
Thanks to that, Hiranuma had spent the time up until a short while ago rushing from room to room to explain the situation to each patient.
After this there was also the matter of arranging a repair service, and today would surely end in overtime just for the inspection...
"I'm sorry to come at such a busy time..."
"No, don't worry about it.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2017. ...Anyway, the gardening club is an unusual one, isn't it? Do you actually grow flowers in flower beds, then?"
"Yes.@k@vS11/35/420500020. My mother runs a flower arrangement class, and I love flowers too..."
"What a lovely thing to do.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2020. I wonder if you'll follow the same path as your mother, Ozaki-san?"

"Oh — I'm not sure...@k@vS11/35/420500022. My mother says I should take my time deciding after I get to university.@k@vS11/35/420500023. Apparently it doesn't make much money, the kind of hobby class she runs."

The girl shrugged and tilted her head with a wry smile. ...Indeed, turning a profit on a housewife's hobby class without something on a large scale or with a genuinely innovative angle would be a difficult proposition.
And in these times, it was perhaps natural for a parent to want their daughter to have a respectable academic record and secure as stable a future as possible.
"Do you go to cram school as well, Ozaki-san?"

"Yes. Actually, tomorrow being Sunday, there's a level-advancement test.@k@vS11/35/420500025. If I pass this time, I'll be moved to more advanced material than what they cover in school — which would give me an advantage on entrance exams."
"Entrance exams... isn't that still quite a way off?"
"If I do well from now, I'll be free to aim for whatever school I choose.@k@vS11/35/420500027. ...High school might be out of reach, but I'd really like to go to the same university as Kouhei-kun..."
"Is that so. Do your best."
In that quiet reply... a faint, firm edge.
It was the expression of a quiet rage against the unreasonable circumstances that had placed her apart from the person she loved.

"But in that case... ah, sorry, am I being too presumptuous?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2025. Doesn't it cost quite a lot to take the bullet train here every week?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2026. The fares alone..."
"It's fine.@k@vS11/35/420500030. My parents are covering the fares.@k@vS11/35/420500031. ...It was a rather sudden move, so they said they'd at least let me have this."
"My... what kind parents."
"...They worry about me.@k@vS11/35/420500033. No... maybe frightened is the word. Frightened I might end up like that girl."

"That if my frustrations build up, I might snap.@k@vS11/35/420500036. So now, even if I come home late, they don't scold me.@k@vS11/35/420500037. They say, 'We trust you...' — with frightened eyes when they say it."
"Being treated so 'gently,' like something that might shatter — it's honestly more of a shock."
"I see..."
Something must have happened at home, Hiranuma thought as she kept nodding along.
Even though they saw each other every week, this was the first time Nagisa had really opened up about herself, about her own circumstances.
(...I wonder if someone in her family said something to her.)
Holding that thought, Hiranuma had just begun to open her mouth to probe gently — when,
"...Hiranuma-san."
The smile vanished from Nagisa's face, and she stopped in place. Following her lead, Hiranuma also stopped and turned to look at her with a puzzled expression.
"What's wrong...?"
"Umm... how has Kouhei-kun been, since that day?"
"...He's doing well, yes.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2033. His eyesight seems to be improving too. He may be discharged fairly soon."
"No, that isn't... what I was asking about."
At Nagisa's question, Hiranuma hesitated before answering.
(...That matter.)
She let out a small sigh, then turned to face the girl and asked.
"Hey, Ozaki-san.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2037. Is it really true what you mentioned — that Sawamura-kun said strange things in his sleep?"
At the question put to her as if to reconfirm, Nagisa's eyes shifted around uncertainly, and she answered with a reluctant nod.

"It was last week, when Kouhei-kun had a high fever and was in bed.@k@vS11/35/420500044. ...He was like someone in the grip of a nightmare, saying, 'Oyashiro-sama is coming, save me.'@k@vS11/35/420500045. ...I heard it clearly."
"...And what did he say when you asked him about it?"
"...@k@vS11/35/420500047. 'I don't know anything about that.' When I brought it up with his primary doctor, I got the same answer."
"...But it really happened. He was holding my hand when he said it.@k@vS11/35/420500050. And yet nobody believes me..."

Nagisa bit her lip, her shoulders dropping. ...She didn't seem to be lying, but there was deep disappointment in her expression — the look of someone whose account had not been believed by a single person she had told.
"Oyashiro-sama."
Honestly, it was a name she had no memory of having heard before. She'd heard it whispered that it was the name of a deity worshipped in the folk religion of some distant village... but.

"...It's a strange business, isn't it.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2040. A word the person themselves doesn't know appearing in their sleep.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2041. His attending physician and the psychosomatic medicine doctor both said that particular word never came up in any of his consultations either... I wonder what it means."
"I want to know too!@k@vS11/35/420500052. Why on earth would it happen?@k@vS11/35/420500053. Why would Kouhei-kun say 'Oyashiro-sama' in a dream — a name he shouldn't even know...!"
"...Ah, I'm sorry.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2043. I didn't mean it as a suggestion I doubted you. Really."
Nagisa's voice had taken on a heated edge. Hiranuma soothed her and continued.
"Only — it does seem strange, that a word the person themselves doesn't know would come out like that.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2045. Could he have heard it somewhere? From someone?"
"...It's not the occult, so there must be some explanation.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2047. That's all I want to understand."
"Is there... anything that comes to mind?"
"...@k@vS11/35/420500056. In that case... it was definitely her. She must have put those words into Kouhei-kun's head...!"
With those words, Nagisa's face flooded with undisguised hatred, and she ground her teeth. ...The bouquet pressed to her chest was being crushed, and several petals scattered to the floor.
"That girl... the one who transferred away? Your friend?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2050. Her name was, let me think..."
"...She is not my friend!@k@vS11/35/420500059. She did something terrible to Kouhei-kun and the others — she...!"
The girl spat it out in a low, heavy voice that was almost a groan. ...Knowing the "circumstances" behind the boy's hospitalization, all Hiranuma could do was sigh.
...Six months ago.
A girl at a certain school had committed an act of violence. It didn't make the newspapers, but it was notorious enough in the surrounding area that Hiranuma had heard about the horrifying scene.
After school, with a metal bat in hand, the girl had suddenly turned on her close friends and beaten them indiscriminately.
One of those friends — Nagisa's boyfriend Kouhei — was unlucky enough to sustain injuries severe enough to leave him on the edge of blindness.

After that, she continued her rampage through the school building — smashing windows in the hallways and classrooms, striking lockers and walls — until several physical education teachers finally managed to restrain her.
The cause of the outburst was discussed at length afterward: exam stress, issues in her personal relationships, and various other factors were offered up as likely triggers.
However, since the girl's own account was never disclosed to the victims' side, there was nothing to establish the truth.
The school's decision to shut down the flow of information entirely — driven by concerns about its reputation — was another major factor.

That was not merely out of consideration for the students; it was also to avoid unnecessary anxiety and misunderstanding among the parents of exam-year students at a sensitive time. In a sense it was a natural response.
Further, because not only the perpetrator but the victims themselves went silent, the incident was in the process of being quietly buried as a thing of the past...
"Reina Ryuugu-san... was that right?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2053. You were all close, weren't you — one group of friends."
"...@k@vS11/35/420500061. In the end, she transferred to somewhere without saying a single word of apology."
"Even when I asked the teachers where she went, no one would tell me.@k@vS11/35/420500063. Even though Kouhei-kun suffered an injury he may never fully recover from...!"
"...Ozaki-san."
"I can never forgive her... If I see her again, I'll... I'll..."
"Calm down.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2056. ...If Sawamura-kun sees you looking like that, he'll worry, you know?"
"Huh? ...Oh."
The girl seemed to finally come back to herself at those words. She drew a sharp breath and looked down. Noticing the bouquet had been somewhat crushed, she hurried to straighten it.
"Ozaki-san."

"I understand how you feel... but even if you stay angry and full of hatred, she's no longer here.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2059. Wouldn't it be better to put your energy into what you can do for the people who matter to you right now?"
"That's what would make Sawamura-kun happy, I'm sure."
"So let's drop the subject for now.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2062. He might have picked that word up from a book or television without even realizing it. Reading too much into it will only hurt you."
Nagisa gave a faint, small nod in response.
But the look on her face made it clear she was not yet persuaded.
Before long, the two of them arrived at a hospital room near the far end of the hallway. On the nameplate beside the door, "Kouhei Sawamura" was written in marker.
Hiranuma rapped lightly on the door — knock, knock. And then,
"...Sawamura-san?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2064. Your girlfriend is here to see you."
"Hm? Was I wrong?"
"Uh, no, umm... ah..."
The girl's face went scarlet, and she hid it behind the bouquet.
Hiranuma watched the innocent reaction with a gentle smile... but something nagged at her, and she turned back to the door with a slight frown.
He would normally answer right away. There was no response.
"Hmm, has he fallen asleep again?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2068. We're coming in...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2069. Huh?"
She said a brief word of warning and reached for the doorknob — and her unease shifted to suspicion. ...It was locked.
"That's strange. Why would it be..."
"...Hiranuma-san?"
"Ah, one moment. Let me just..."
She pulled the keychain from her pocket and fitted one of the keys into the lock.
...All the private rooms in this hospital had locks installed, due to "certain circumstances."
Patients could lock their doors from the inside, but the on-duty nurse's master key could override them.
However, this particular patient had never once locked his door since being moved to this room last week... so why?
"Umm... maybe I should come back another time."
"It's all right. I'll explain everything."
"...Sawamura-san, I'm coming in.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2074. Excuse me."
With those words, Hiranuma opened the door to the hospital room and stepped inside.
"...Kouhei-kun?"
But... the room was empty.
The duvet was half pushed aside in a way that made the recent presence of someone in the bed palpable.
In the silent room, the only sound was the faint shuffling of a canary in its cage — placed there to comfort the patient during his stay.
"Where could he have gone?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2077. If he had left the room, the door shouldn't have been locked.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2078. ...Oh — the window is left open."
"Ah, I'll close it."
The late-afternoon breeze gusted in, setting the beige curtains rippling.
The girl set her bouquet on the table, padded quietly toward the window, and leaned forward to take hold of the handle...
"— No, nooooo!!"
"What?! Wh-what's wrong, Ozaki-san?!"
"Ah... aaah... aaaaahh!!"
The girl remained frozen in place, leaning over the window sill, her face drained white and trembling. Hiranuma rushed to her side — and following her gaze, looked out—
"S-Sawamura-kun?!"
...Far below, on the ground, a boy in pajamas lay spread-eagled on his back.
And spreading from around his body was something clearly unlike the shadow of the setting sun — staining the gray concrete a deep, dark black—!
"Kouhei-kun!@k@vS11/35/420500079. Kouhei-kun!!@k@vS11/35/420500080. Aaah, aaaaaahh!!"
"Ozaki-san, it's dangerous!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2082. You could fall too!!"
"Kouhei-kun, Kouhei-kun!!@k@vS11/35/420500082. Uwaaahh!! Noooo!!"
Hiranuma caught the screaming girl and pulled her back from the window, retreating to the wall on the far side of the room.
Then she seized the nurse-call receiver mounted nearby and began shouting into it without waiting for a reply.

"Hello, nurse station!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2084. The patient in room 842 has fallen from the window!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2085. Contact the police immediately!!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2086. ...And call Dr. Toda, the attending physician, right away!!"
...The scene seen from an eighth-floor window. A sight beyond miserable.
That distance was the boundary between the living and the dead.
"Huh? ...An interview?"
"That's right."
Called in immediately upon returning from lunch, and unwillingly entering the chief's office, Tomoe found Chief Yamaoki delivering that as the very first words out of his mouth.

"It's a formal request from the National Police Agency's public relations office.@k@vS11/30/420200003. A programming proposal from one of the major commercial networks — they say your cooperation is essential.@k@vS11/30/420200004. You can probably imagine the general idea."
"...More or less."
Shrugging with a touch of resignation, Tomoe muttered inwardly: not again.
Ever since making a name for herself as a detective, no matter how her rank or posting changed, requests of this sort kept flooding in relentlessly.
Lately, television appearances had been particularly common.
It was understandable — increasing the appearances of female officers would generate better ratings than staging dramatic arrests with the rough, jock-type image that police were known for.
Her objection wasn't to the programming format — it was to the theme.
(Expanding women's rights... not really in my wheelhouse.)
She glanced through the proposal handed to her from the network and sighed.

The program appeared to be something along the lines of a success story — a grandly dramatized account of how a female police officer came to be selected for the National Police Agency's recommended track from the Ibaraki prefectural police. ...Apparently.

In short, it was intended to trumpet the trend of equal employment opportunity between men and women that had been fashionable of late. ...Honestly, it wasn't something she felt enthused about at all.
(In reality, the differences between men and women aren't anything surprising...)

Tomoe didn't embrace the notion that "women can be capable too" or "stop discriminating" as some bold rallying cry. It just happened to be that she was a woman — that was as far as her thinking went on the matter.
Besides, the reason Tomoe threw herself into investigative work day and night was to pursue opportunities to get closer to the mystery her father had left behind.

The idea of taking that and turning it into a public statement about expanding women's rights — speaking as a representative of working women everywhere... It seemed nothing short of presumptuous, and the mere thought of it made her skin crawl.
...Which was why Tomoe's answer was the same each time, without fail.
"Well, as for the matter of your appearance..."
"I decline."
No preamble whatsoever.
"This is the first time the request has come through the public relations office, but my reason is unchanged.@k@vS11/28/420100101. Even if I appear on television, I have nothing meaningful to say."
"...Hmm.@k@vS11/30/420200007. Well, I don't think it calls for quite such a formal refusal."
"Besides, my position involves active investigations. Having my face known to the public would only complicate those activities.@k@vS11/28/420100103. I'm grateful for the offer, but I must decline."
"...You're always so rigid, you know.@k@vS11/29/420400101. Nobody is going to remember you from a single television appearance."
"It's only natural for a detective to be cautious.@k@vS11/28/420100105. ...And Madoka — why are you here?"
She had been vaguely aware of something odd ever since she came in, and finally turned to ask directly.
Before Tomoe had entered, Madoka and Fujita had been standing at the wall of the chief's office for some reason.
"Do you have some business with the chief?@k@vS11/28/420100107. If this doesn't involve you, you are on duty — I'd like you to step out."
"Well, we were called here.@k@vS11/29/420400103. Right, Fujita-san?"
"Ah, well... I honestly don't know why I was called here either..."
Fujita said this with an apologetic scratch of his head, but his face was bright.

It turned out that he had knelt in the doorway of his wife's family home and pled his case about the misunderstanding from the other day — had actually, literally groveled — and had finally been forgiven.
As a result, the moment he arrived at work this morning, Tomoe had been treated to a full account of it in all its joyful, lovesick detail.
They themselves took it all very seriously, but from the outside it was all rather trivial — an annoyance beyond measure.
She had been silently thinking "...Go to hell" as she chomped through her lunch hamburgers.
"A business trip to Tokyo, lucky.@k@vS11/29/420400105. Bring me back a souvenir, Sis♪"
"I said I'm not going.@k@vS11/28/420100109. ...Please just convey my refusal.@k@vS11/28/420100110. I have more than enough cases on my hands right now and no capacity for anything else."
A television appearance would mean suspending ongoing investigative processes for the duration, and making up for it afterward with unpaid overtime — there was genuinely no upside.
Unlike Madoka, who was carelessly overjoyed at the prospect, Tomoe had no room in her packed daily schedule for any of it, regardless of the investigative inconvenience.
"Hmm, that's a problem."
"Huh? Why?"
"Well... because I've already agreed to it."
"Huuuuhh?!"
Abandoning all composure, Tomoe let out an undignified yelp.

"Here, this is the business trip authorization.@k@vS11/30/420200011. Unfortunately there's no first-class carriage, as the public relations office covers expenses — but you do have a reserved seat on the bullet train—"
"W-wait a moment, please!@k@vS11/28/420100114. Why would you accept it? And why without my permission?!"
"Well... if I had asked, you would have refused."
"That's not the point!!@k@vS11/28/420100116. What about my wishes?!@k@vS11/28/420100117. My rights?!@k@vS11/28/420100118. And by what authority did you say 'yes' to any of this?!"
"Well, to put it plainly — and just between us —@k@vS11/30/420200014. ...I am the chief. Which makes me your superior."
"You don't need to say it like it's some secret!@k@vS11/28/420100120. And even if you are my superior, you have approval authority — not decision-making authority!!"
"Is that so?"

"Don't just go along with that!!@k@vS11/28/420100122. — I am absolutely not doing this, I refuse entirely!!@k@vS11/28/420100123. What were they even thinking, putting an active-duty detective on television?@k@vS11/28/420100124. It will make undercover operations impossible going forward?!"
She slammed her hands down on the expensive desk with a loud bang, leaning forward and raising her voice.
Any ordinary detective... no, even a violent criminal would tremble before that expression — Fujita muttered something to that effect to himself, with the poor taste that comes from knowing her.

Yet somehow — whether by disposition or long experience — Yamaoki did not flinch in the slightest at her volume, and continued unhurriedly polishing the golf club he had been holding since they arrived.
"Are you even listening, Chief?!"

"Yes, I'm listening perfectly well.@k@vS11/30/420200017. Ah — I'm afraid age has done something to my hearing of late.@k@vS11/30/420200018. About this level of volume is just right for me.@k@vS11/30/420200019. ...Now, what was it you were saying?"
"Listen to me properly!!"
"S-Sis!@k@vS11/29/420400107. Calm down... let's just calm down, all right?"
Madoka threw herself between Tomoe and the chief, desperately trying to soothe her as she snarled and looked ready to bite.
If she couldn't stop her here, it was hard to say when the verbal assault might turn into something physical...
"W-would you stop it, Kaoru-sa... I mean, Chief!@k@vS11/29/420400109. Please stop winding up Chief Inspector Minai like this!"
"My apologies, Minai-kun.@k@vS11/30/420200021. I was only trying to lighten the mood a bit."
"You've only made it hotter with my anger!!"
"M-Minai-san, break, break!@k@vS11/31/421200006. Please don't get so worked up!!"
Apparently reluctant to lay hands on a woman, Fujita kept a careful distance while trying to calm her.
With both of them holding her back... she seemed to recover a measure of composure at last, straightening her collar and taking a deep breath.
"In any case, I refuse.@k@vS11/28/420100129. If you prefer, I will contact the television station directly myself."

"Now, listen to me, Minai-kun.@k@vS11/30/420200023. Police officers are civil servants — public servants sustained by the people's taxes.@k@vS11/30/420200024. That being so, it goes without saying that we have a responsibility to continuously demonstrate to the public that our work is meeting the expected standard."

"Furthermore, press cooperation is indispensable in large-scale investigations.@k@vS11/30/420200026. For that to be possible, I believe we must maintain a good relationship with them in ordinary times.@k@vS11/30/420200027. ...Don't you agree?"
"W-well... that..."
Those were fair arguments. ...She couldn't exactly bring herself to agree, coming from this particular chief's mouth.

"But surely that's the responsibility of the public relations division?@k@vS11/28/420100132. And I still don't see how exposing an active detective's face to the general public could possibly lead to anything good!"
"Oh, no one will remember a face from a single appearance.@k@vS11/30/420200029. If the public had that kind of memory for faces, wanted criminals would be caught in the blink of an eye."

"What about people who record it?!@k@vS11/28/420100134. And there are reruns too, aren't there?@k@vS11/28/420100135. On top of that, there are real risks and problems in exposing personal information or operational methods to television networks—@k@vS11/28/420100136. ...Hm?"
At that moment, Tomoe's gaze drifted to the golf club Yamaoki had been polishing this whole time, and a memory surfaced.
It was brand new, and she was fairly certain it matched the imported titanium driver she had recently seen advertised on television.
But... she was quite sure that model's Japanese release was still a considerable time away. And yet here it was, already in his possession — which would mean...?!
"Ch-Chief...?@k@vS11/28/420100138. Don't tell me—?!"
"Ah — you know, when I gave them the go-ahead for your appearance, the network people mentioned a small token of gratitude.@k@vS11/30/420200031. ...Nice piece, isn't it?"
*snap*
"I'll — I'll kill you!!@k@vS11/28/420100140. You sold me out for a single golf cluuub?!!"
"M-Minai-san?! S-stop!!"@r@t"S-Sis?! S-stop!!"
With those absolutely alarming words, Tomoe had stopped shouting and was climbing over the desk with every intention of hitting him — and Madoka and Fujita barely managed to restrain her in time.
"...What's that?@k@vS11/30/420200033. You wanted this driver for yourself?"
"Yes!!@k@vS11/28/420100142. Hand it over, right now!!@k@vS11/28/420100143. I'll crack your skull open with it and sink it straight into the pond out baaaack!!!"

"M-Minai-san?!@k@vS11/31/421200009. No bloodshed, please!!@k@vS11/31/421200010. Besides, if you cause any more of a scene in the station, you'll seriously face disciplinary action!!@k@|@y@vS11/31/421200011. ...Ooghf?!"
"If I'm the one getting disciplined, then what about him?!@k@vS11/28/420100145. He's committing bribery right before our eyes!!"
She certainly... had a point.
"Fujita-kun! I'll take responsibility, so hurry up and arrest this criminal!!@k@vS11/28/420100147. Then I'll personally file charges with the district prosecutor's office and throw him behind bars!!"
"W-wait, Sis, please!!@k@|@y@vS11/29/420400112. ...Nkyaaahh?!"
At this point, Fujita could no longer afford to worry about propriety when it came to touching a woman.
If, heaven forbid, the chief were to get injured here, never mind the incident report — personnel affairs would absolutely not let this slide.
And precisely because of that, neither of them wanted her career tarnished over something so idiotic.

"Let go, Fujita-kun!@k@vS11/28/420100149. You've got the soul of a detective too, don't you?@k@vS11/28/420100150. If you have even a shred of a sense of justice, how can you turn a blind eye to corruption happening right in front of you?!"
"W-well, I do think it's a bit much, honestly..."
Or rather... no matter how you tried to spin it, this was unquestionably a bribe.
If Deputy Chief Hata — who was constantly on the hunt for anything to use against the chief or Tomoe — ever caught wind of this, it would be catastrophic...
"Hmm.@k@vS11/30/420200035. ...Sergeant Fujita, are you siding with Minai-kun?"
"Huh?!@k@vS11/31/421200014. S-sides? It's not about sides — I'm just speaking from basic common sense here..."
"Then I suggest you persuade Minai-kun yourself.@k@vS11/30/420200037. ...Using that common sense of yours."
"Huh?"
Suddenly finding himself in the crosshairs, Fujita turned a bewildered face toward Yamaoki, all while struggling to hold down the thrashing Tomoe.

Persuade her? ...He hadn't even wanted to be called in as the one to hold her back, and now this? 'Why me?' was the only thought in his head. And no matter how he looked at it, no matter how he thought about it, Tomoe was undeniably in the right...
"W-well, in terms of common sense...@k@vS11/31/421200017. ...I do think that accepting benefits like that is, well, not exactly proper."

"...Then let me rephrase the question.@k@vS11/30/420200039. If you defy me, I will personally see to it through personnel affairs that your raise next term is delayed.@k@vS11/30/420200040. Your bonus and performance reviews will need revisiting as well."
"...And when that happens and your wife demands to know the reason...@k@vS11/30/420200042. ...do you think she'll forgive you?"
D-... dirty!! Striking at the most critical weak point a married man had?!

A-and on top of that, the last incident had only just blown over — if his bonus got cut again because of something involving Tomoe... there was no question about it — this time, he was absolutely done for!!
"Th-th-th-that's...?!"
"F-Fujita-kun...?!"

"With that in mind, let me ask you once more.@k@vS11/30/420200044. Will you persuade Inspector Minai... or will you prostrate yourself before your wife again?@k@vS11/30/420200045. One or the other... the choice is yours."
"Ugh, urgh...!!"
What's more, where had he even gotten that information? Somehow he even knew about the time Fujita had prostrated himself at his wife's parents' house...
"Gh... guooooooohh...!!"
...Under normal circumstances, Fujita would agonize and agonize before ultimately making the painful choice to side with "justice."
But today's Fujita had tasted, just the night before, something he absolutely never wanted to experience a second time in his life.
And so... he caved in with stunning ease.
"M-Minai-san...!@k@vS11/31/421200023. Please, I'm begging you...@k@vS11/31/421200024. ...just do the interview...!"
"Huuuuuhh?!@k@vS11/28/420100153. Fujita, you're selling out your own superior?!"
"I'm sorry, I'm so sorry!@k@vS11/31/421200026. I just can't grovel on the ground in front of my wife's family again!!"
"It breaks something inside you, seriously!!@k@vS11/31/421200028. You feel so wretched and pathetic, like your entire worth as a person is being denied!!!"
"You... you spineless wretch!!!"
...It was anyone's guess which of the two men that insult was aimed at.
"Now, now, do calm down, Minai-kun.@k@vS11/30/420200047. You're misunderstanding the situation."
"Misunderstanding what?!"

"This is not a bribe — it's simply a gift.@k@vS11/30/420200049. I never demanded it, nor did I grant any special favors in exchange.@k@vS11/30/420200050. ...Well, we happened to be chatting about golf while flipping through a magazine, and I may have said, 'Now that's a fine club.'"
"Normal people call that extortion, you shameless old geezer!!@k@vS11/28/420100157. And you absolutely did grant favors — you volunteered me without my permission!!"

"No, no — that's merely a difference in personal interpretation.@k@vS11/30/420200052. ...At any rate, the message has been delivered. Make sure to keep your schedule clear next week.@k@vS11/30/420200053. ...That will be all."
"Don't screw with meee!!!@k@vS11/28/420100159. Hey — get back heeere!!"
With that, Yamaoki strolled out of his office without a care in the world, leaving Tomoe raging in his wake.
"Heeeeey, don't you run from me, you damn Chief!!@k@vS11/28/420100161. Graaaaaaahh!!"
"M-Minai-san, please calm down!!"
"...Ow ow ow ow?!@k@vS11/31/421200031. M-Minai-san, t-time out!!@k@vS11/31/421200032. Stop putting me in joint locks!!@k@vS11/31/421200033. Gya, gyaaaaaaa?!"
"I-it's gonna break, it's gonna break! This time it's not my heart — it's my arm that's about to snap!!"
"H-hello, First Investigation Division! Is Takayashiki-san or Hanada-san there?@k@vS11/29/420400114. ...Oh, Andou-san is there too?"
"This is an emergency! Everyone to the Hall of Pine —@k@vS11/29/420400116. — wait, no, to the Chief's office!!@k@vS11/29/420400117. Assistant Director Minai has lost it!!"
"Son of a biiiiitch!!!"
Some days later, a certain detective summed up the entire incident in a single remark.
"...If someone told me to choose between restraining Minai-san and being a lion keeper, I'd pick the lions without a second thought."
